Operation 'cloudscheduler.projects.locations.jobs.list' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is extremely vague with no context about what is returned or what inputs are needed. Added clarity about the parent parameter requirement, pagination support, return type (Job resources), and the location-based scope. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.summary_suggestion: List scheduled jobs in a location
data.description_suggestion: Lists all jobs in the specified location. Returns a paginated list of Job resources with their configurations and current state. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Requires the parent location path in the format 'projects/{project}/locations/{location}'.
data.reasoning: Original description is extremely vague with no context about what is returned or what inputs are needed. Added clarity about the parent parameter requirement, pagination support, return type (Job resources), and the location-based scope. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'cloudscheduler.projects.locations.jobs.create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description lacks essential details for AI agent decision-making. Enhanced with: input requirements (Job resource with configuration details), size constraint (1MB max), output details (created Job with system-generated name), and side effects (job begins executing per schedule). Added domain context about job configuration components and initial state to improve interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new job in the specified location. Accepts a Job resource containing the job configuration (schedule, target, retry settings, etc.). The maximum allowed job size is 1MB. Returns the created Job resource with a system-generated name and initial state. The job will begin executing according to its schedule after creation.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks essential details for AI agent decision-making. Enhanced with: input requirements (Job resource with configuration details), size constraint (1MB max), output details (created Job with system-generated name), and side effects (job begins executing per schedule). Added domain context about job configuration components and initial state to improve interpretability.

message: Operation 'cloudscheduler.projects.locations.jobs.delete' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Original description is too minimal. Added context about what deletion means (removes from schedule), what input is required (job name), what is returned (empty response), and error handling. This helps AI agents understand the operation's purpose and effects.

data.description_suggestion: Deletes a job and removes it from the schedule. The job will no longer execute on its recurring schedule. Requires the job name/resource identifier. Returns an empty response on successful deletion. If the job does not exist, a NOT_FOUND error is returned.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added context about what deletion means (removes from schedule), what input is required (job name), what is returned (empty response), and error handling. This helps AI agents understand the operation's purpose and effects.

message: Operation 'cloudscheduler.projects.locations.jobs.get' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original description lacks specificity about what data is returned and what input is needed. Enhanced to clarify that it returns a Job object with configuration and state, requires a job identifier, and handles the not-found case.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the configuration and current state of a specific job. Requires the job's resource name/identifier. Returns the complete Job object containing schedule, target configuration, state, and execution history. If the job does not exist, a NOT_FOUND error is returned.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what data is returned and what input is needed. Enhanced to clarify that it returns a Job object with configuration and state, requires a job identifier, and handles the not-found case.

message: Operation 'cloudscheduler.projects.locations.list' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description is generic and lacks specificity about what is returned. Enhanced to clarify that it returns location metadata, mentions pagination support, and provides context about the purpose (where jobs can be created).

data.current_description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service.
data.description_suggestion: Lists all supported locations where Cloud Scheduler jobs can be created and managed. Returns a ListLocationsResponse containing available locations with their display names and metadata. Supports pagination through filter, pageSize, and pageToken parameters.
data.reasoning: Original description is generic and lacks specificity about what is returned. Enhanced to clarify that it returns location metadata, mentions pagination support, and provides context about the purpose (where jobs can be created).

message: Operation 'cloudscheduler.projects.locations.jobs.run' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description lacks context about concurrent execution behavior and what is returned. Enhanced to clarify the side effect (concurrent executions possible), input requirement (job name), output (Job object), and use cases. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Forces a job to run now. When this method is called, Cloud Scheduler will dispatch the job, even if the job is already running.
data.summary_suggestion: Trigger immediate execution of a job
data.description_suggestion: Forces a job to execute immediately, bypassing its normal schedule. The job will be dispatched even if it is already running, potentially creating concurrent executions. Requires the job's resource name. Returns the Job object with updated execution status. Useful for testing or urgent job execution.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks context about concurrent execution behavior and what is returned. Enhanced to clarify the side effect (concurrent executions possible), input requirement (job name), output (Job object), and use cases. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation.
